from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction began in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "capital is",
        "language": "language is",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Collect objects per predicate (allowing multiple values)
        pred_objs = defaultdict(list)
        for tr in group:
            pred_objs[tr.predicate].append(tr.object)

        clause_parts = []

        # Helper to format a list of objects nicely
        def fmt_list(objs):
            if not objs:
                return ""
            if len(objs) == 1:
                return objs[0]
            return ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]

        # Capital / location / country style predicates
        if "capital" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"the capital of {subject} is {fmt_list(pred_objs['capital'])}")
        if "location" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} is located in {fmt_list(pred_objs['location'])}")
        if "country" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} is in {fmt_list(pred_objs['country'])}")
        if "region" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} is in the region of {fmt_list(pred_objs['region'])}")
        if "state" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} is in the state of {fmt_list(pred_objs['state'])}")

        # Architectural attributes
        if "architecturalStyle" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} has an architectural style of {fmt_list(pred_objs['architecturalStyle'])}")
        if "architect" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} was designed by {fmt_list(pred_objs['architect'])}")
        if "height" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} rises to {fmt_list(pred_objs['height'])}")
        if "floorCount" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} has {fmt_list(pred_objs['floorCount'])} floors")
        if "floorArea" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} covers a floor area of {fmt_list(pred_objs['floorArea'])}")
        if "buildingType" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} is a {fmt_list(pred_objs['buildingType'])}")

        # Ownership / development
        if "owner" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} is owned by {fmt_list(pred_objs['owner'])}")
        if "developer" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} was developed by {fmt_list(pred_objs['developer'])}")
        if "tenant" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} has a tenant {fmt_list(pred_objs['tenant'])}")
        if "currentTenants" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} currently houses {fmt_list(pred_objs['currentTenants'])}")

        # Cost / date
        if "cost" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} costs {fmt_list(pred_objs['cost'])}")
        if "buildingStartDate" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"construction of {subject} began in {fmt_list(pred_objs['buildingStartDate'])}")
        if "completionDate" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} was completed in {fmt_list(pred_objs['completionDate'])}")
        if "inaugurationDate" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} was inaugurated on {fmt_list(pred_objs['inaugurationDate'])}")

        # Ethnic groups and language
        if "ethnicGroup" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"One of the ethnic groups of {subject} is {fmt_list(pred_objs['ethnicGroup'])}")
        if "language" in pred_objs:
            clause_parts.append(f"The language of {subject} is {fmt_list(pred_objs['language'])}")

        # Leadership
        titles = pred_objs.get("leaderTitle", [])
        leaders = pred_objs.get("leader", [])
        if titles and leaders:
            for lt, ld in zip(titles, leaders):
                clause_parts.append(f"The {lt} of {subject} is {ld}")
        elif leaders:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} has leader {fmt_list(leaders)}")
        elif titles:
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} has leader title {fmt_list(titles)}")

        # Miscellaneous predicates using generic verb_map
        generic_handled = {"capital","location","country","region","state","architecturalStyle","architect","height","floorCount","floorArea","buildingType","owner","developer","tenant","currentTenants","cost","buildingStartDate","completionDate","inaugurationDate","ethnicGroup","language","leaderTitle","leader"}
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            if pred in generic_handled:
                continue
            verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
            clause_parts.append(f"{subject} {verb} {fmt_list(objs)}")

        # Combine clauses into a single sentence
        if clause_parts:
            sentence = "; ".join(clause_parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = subject + "."

        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize first character of the whole output
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
